days.title: Week 3, Day 1: Asking for Directions

words.example_sentence_native: Where is the train station?
Turn left at the traffic light.
The pharmacy is on the right.
Go straight ahead.

words.example_sentence_target: Este un magazin aproape de aici?
words.example_sentence_native: Is there a store near here?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Yes-teh oon ma-ga-zeen a-pro-ah-peh deh a-eech?

dialogues.context: A tourist asking a local for directions to the museum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Scuzați-mă, unde este muzeul?
lines.native: Excuse me, where is the museum?
lines.phonetic: Skoo-zahts-muh, oon-deh yes-teh moo-zeh-ool?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Mergeți drept înainte și apoi faceți la dreapta. Este foarte aproape.
lines.native: Go straight ahead and then turn right. It is very near.
lines.phonetic: Mer-jetz drept uh-na-in-teh shee a-poy fa-chetz la dreyap-ta. Yes-teh foar-teh a-pro-ah-peh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bună ziua! Caut o farmacie.
lines.native: Hello! I'm looking for a pharmacy.
lines.phonetic: Boo-nuh zee-wa! Cowt o far-ma-chee-eh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Este una pe dreapta, după colț.
lines.native: There is one on the right, around the corner.
lines.phonetic: Yes-teh oo-na peh dreyap-ta, doo-puh kolts.

days.cultural_note: Romanians are generally very friendly and willing to help with directions, even if they don't speak much English. Using a few Romanian words like 'Scuzați-mă' will be much appreciated.
days.pronunciation_tip: The letter 'ț' in 'faceți' sounds like 'ts' in 'cats'. It's a sharp, quick sound.

days.title: Week 3, Day 2: Public Transport

words.example_sentence_native: Which bus goes to the center?

words.example_sentence_target: Unde este cea mai apropiată stație de autobuz?
words.example_sentence_native: Where is the nearest bus stop?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Oon-deh yes-teh cha my a-pro-pee-a-tuh sta-tsee-eh deh ow-to-booz?

words.example_sentence_native: I want a ticket, please.
The train leaves at ten o'clock.
We can take the subway.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bună ziua, un bilet pentru centru, vă rog.
lines.native: Hello, one ticket to the center, please.
lines.phonetic: Boo-nuh zee-wa, oon bee-let pen-troo chen-troo, vuh rog.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Sigur. Poftiți.
lines.native: Sure. Here you are.
lines.phonetic: See-goor. Pof-teets.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Când pleacă următorul tren spre Brașov?
lines.native: When does the next train to Brașov leave?
lines.phonetic: Kund ple-a-cuh oor-muh-tor-ool tren spreh Bra-shov?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: În zece minute.
lines.native: In ten minutes.
lines.phonetic: In ze-cheh mee-noo-teh.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'stație'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Stație' can mean both a bus stop or a train/metro station.

days.cultural_note: In many Romanian cities like Bucharest, you need to buy a 'card' for public transport which you top up with credit, rather than buying single paper tickets for each journey.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'ă' sound in 'stație' or 'apropiată' is a neutral vowel sound, similar to the 'a' in 'sofa' in English.

days.title: Week 3, Day 3: Taxi & Ride-sharing

words.example_sentence_target: Pot să iau un taxi de aici?
words.example_sentence_native: Can I get a taxi from here?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Pot suh yow oon tak-see deh a-eech?

words.example_sentence_native: What is the address?
How much does it cost to the airport?

words.target: Vă rog să mă duceți la...
words.phonetic: Vuh rog suh muh doo-chetz la...
words.example_sentence_target: Vă rog să mă duceți la acest hotel.
words.example_sentence_native: Please take me to this hotel.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Vuh rog suh muh doo-chetz la a-chest ho-tel.

words.example_sentence_native: Please, stop here.

dialogues.context: Taking a taxi to a specific address.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bună ziua. Vă rog să mă duceți la Strada Victoriei, numărul 10.
lines.native: Hello. Please take me to 10 Victory Street.
lines.phonetic: Boo-nuh zee-wa. Vuh rog suh muh doo-chetz la Stra-da Vik-to-ree-ey, noo-muh-rool ze-cheh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Sigur, nicio problemă.
lines.native: Sure, no problem.
lines.phonetic: See-goor, nee-cho pro-bleh-muh.

dialogues.context: Asking the price before getting in a taxi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Cât costă aproximativ până la gară?
lines.native: How much is it approximately to the train station?
lines.phonetic: Kut kos-tuh a-prok-see-ma-teev puh-nuh la ga-ruh?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: În jur de 30 de lei.
lines.native: Around 30 lei.
lines.phonetic: In joor deh trey-zeci deh lay.

multipleChoice.question: How do you ask 'How much does it cost?'
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Cât costă?' is the correct phrase to ask for the price.

days.cultural_note: Ride-sharing apps like Uber and Bolt are very popular and often more reliable than traditional taxis in major Romanian cities like Bucharest, Cluj-Napoca, and Timișoara.
days.pronunciation_tip: In 'duceți', the 'ce' is pronounced like 'cheh' in 'check'. It's a soft 'ch' sound.

days.title: Week 3, Day 4: Landmarks

words.example_sentence_native: The hotel is on the main street.

words.example_sentence_target: Unde este cea mai apropiată bancă?
words.example_sentence_native: Where is the nearest bank?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Oon-deh yes-teh cha my a-pro-pee-a-tuh ban-kuh?

words.example_sentence_native: I need to go to the hospital.
I know a good restaurant.
We are going to Union Square.

dialogues.context: Asking for directions to a hotel.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Scuzați-mă, este un hotel pe aici?
lines.native: Excuse me, is there a hotel around here?
lines.phonetic: Skoo-zahts-muh, yes-teh oon ho-tel peh a-eech?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Da, Hotel Inter este pe bulevard, la dreapta.
lines.native: Yes, Hotel Inter is on the boulevard, on the right.
lines.phonetic: Da, Ho-tel In-ter yes-teh peh boo-leh-vard, la dreyap-ta.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Urgent! Unde este cel mai apropiat spital?
lines.native: Urgent! Where is the nearest hospital?
lines.phonetic: Oor-jent! Oon-deh yes-teh chel my a-pro-pee-at spee-tal?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Mergeți drept înainte, este la 5 minute de mers pe jos.
lines.native: Go straight ahead, it's a 5-minute walk.
lines.phonetic: Mer-jetz drept uh-na-in-teh, yes-teh la chinch mee-noo-teh deh mers peh zhos.

fillBlanks.hint: I need to go to the hospital.

multipleChoice.question: What does 'piață' mean?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Piață' can mean a market where you buy produce, or a public square like Piața Unirii.

days.cultural_note: In Romania, many important streets are named after historical events or figures. For example, 'Calea Victoriei' (Victory Avenue) in Bucharest is a major historical and cultural hub.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'h' in 'hotel' is pronounced, unlike in French. It's a soft 'h' sound like in English 'hello'.

days.title: Week 3, Day 5: Understanding Directions

words.example_sentence_native: Is it far from here?
The store is next to the bank.
The parking is in front of the hotel.
The garden is behind the house.
The pharmacy is at the corner.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Scuzați-mă, este departe castelul?
lines.native: Excuse me, is the castle far?
lines.phonetic: Skoo-zahts-muh, yes-teh deh-par-teh cas-tel-ool?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Nu, nu este departe. Este la 10 minute de mers pe jos.
lines.native: No, it's not far. It is a 10-minute walk.
lines.phonetic: Noo, noo yes-teh deh-par-teh. Yes-teh la ze-cheh mee-noo-teh deh mers peh zhos.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Unde este cafeneaua?
lines.native: Where is the cafe?
lines.phonetic: Oon-deh yes-teh ca-feh-ne-a-wa?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Este lângă teatru, la colțul străzii.
lines.native: It's next to the theater, at the street corner.
lines.phonetic: Yes-teh lun-guh teh-a-troo, la kol-tsool struh-zee.

fillBlanks.hint: The store is next to the bank.

multipleChoice.question: What is the opposite of 'aproape' (near)?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Departe' means 'far', which is the opposite of 'aproape' (near).

days.cultural_note: In Romanian cities, giving directions using landmarks ('lângă bancă' - next to the bank) is extremely common, sometimes more so than using street names for short distances.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'â' in 'lângă' and 'în' is a unique Romanian vowel. It's a sound made in the center of your mouth, similar to the 'u' in 'uh-oh'.

days.title: Week 3, Day 6: Travel Verbs

words.example_sentence_native: I am going to the market.
Are you coming with me?
I will take the bus.
When do we arrive?
The train is leaving now.

dialogues.context: Making plans to go to the city center.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Mergem în centru. Vii cu noi?
lines.native: We are going to the center. Are you coming with us?
lines.phonetic: Mer-jem in chen-troo. Vee koo noy?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Da, vin! Cum mergem?
lines.native: Yes, I'm coming! How are we going?
lines.phonetic: Da, veen! Koom mer-jem?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Autobuzul pleacă la ora 9. Ajungem la 9:30.
lines.native: The bus leaves at 9. We arrive at 9:30.
lines.phonetic: Ow-to-boo-zool ple-a-cuh la o-ra now-uh. A-joon-jem la now-uh tr-ey-zeci.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Perfect. Voi lua biletul acum.
lines.native: Perfect. I will take (buy) the ticket now.
lines.phonetic: Per-fect. Voy loo-a bee-let-ool a-koom.

sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I am going to the market.

days.cultural_note: In Romanian, 'a lua' (to take) is often used when you mean 'to buy' in specific contexts, like buying a ticket ('a lua un bilet') or getting food to-go.
days.pronunciation_tip: The verb endings change for each person (eu merg, tu mergi, el/ea merge). Pay attention to these small changes as they are very important for meaning.

days.title: Week 3, Day 7: Review

words.example_sentence_target: Faceți la stânga la următoarea stradă.
words.example_sentence_native: Turn left at the next street.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Fa-chetz la stun-ga la oor-muh-toar-e-a stra-duh.

words.example_sentence_native: It's on the right side.

words.example_sentence_target: Am nevoie de un bilet de autobuz.
words.example_sentence_native: I need a bus ticket.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Am neh-voy-eh deh oon bee-let deh ow-to-booz.

words.example_sentence_target: Cât costă un bilet la film?
words.example_sentence_native: How much does a movie ticket cost?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kut kos-tuh oon bee-let la film?

words.example_sentence_native: Where are we going now?

dialogues.context: A full dialogue asking for directions and taking a bus.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Scuzați-mă, cum pot ajunge la Muzeul Național?
lines.native: Excuse me, how can I get to the National Museum?
lines.phonetic: Skoo-zahts-muh, koom pot a-joon-jeh la Moo-zeh-ool Na-tsyo-nal?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Puteți lua autobuzul 381. Stația este la colț, pe dreapta.
lines.native: You can take bus 381. The stop is at the corner, on the right.
lines.phonetic: Poo-tets loo-a ow-to-boo-zool trey-soo-teh op-zeci shee oo-noo. Sta-tsee-a yes-teh la kolts, peh dreyap-ta.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Vă rog să mă duceți la acest hotel. Este departe?
lines.native: Please take me to this hotel. Is it far?
lines.phonetic: Vuh rog suh muh doo-chetz la a-chest ho-tel. Yes-teh deh-par-teh?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Nu, ajungem în aproximativ 15 minute.
lines.native: No, we'll arrive in about 15 minutes.
lines.phonetic: Noo, a-joon-jem in a-prok-see-ma-teev cheen-spreh-ze-cheh mee-noo-teh.

fillBlanks.hint: The stop is at the corner, on the right.

multipleChoice.question: Which verb means 'to leave'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'A pleca' means 'to leave'. 'A veni' is to come, 'a ajunge' is to arrive, 'a lua' is to take.

days.cultural_note: Review: Public transportation is the most common way to get around major cities. For inter-city travel, trains (run by CFR) are a popular and scenic option.
days.pronunciation_tip: Review: Remember the 'ă', 'î', 'â', 'ș', and 'ț' sounds. They are key to being understood in Romanian. Practice them daily!
